introSection: G'sengte Sau is a steel wild mouse roller coaster located at Erlebnispark Tripsdrill in Cleebronn, Baden-Württemberg, Germany. Opened in 1998, it was the first roller coaster to be manufactured by German manufacturer Gerstlauer. The ride's steel was manufactured by Maurer Rides GmbH, as Gerstlauer was still very new to manufacturing at this point.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: After a switchback wild mouse section, a more intense section follows with drops and twists.

sections.title: Etymology
sections.content: G'Sengte Sau is a local dialect from the Tripsdrill region and it simply means "Fast Rider/Driver".

sections.title: Trivia
sections.content: Using specially designed inline skates Dirk Auer (world record holder for traveling on inline skates) rode / skated this coaster track during its opening ceremony while carrying a liter of beer.

fullText:
G'sengte Sau is a steel wild mouse roller coaster located at Erlebnispark Tripsdrill in Cleebronn, Baden-Württemberg, Germany. Opened in 1998, it was the first roller coaster to be manufactured by German manufacturer Gerstlauer. The ride's steel was manufactured by Maurer Rides GmbH, as Gerstlauer was still very new to manufacturing at this point.

After a switchback wild mouse section, a more intense section follows with drops and twists.

Green track and supports.



G'Sengte Sau is a local dialect from the Tripsdrill region and it simply means "Fast Rider/Driver".

Using specially designed inline skates Dirk Auer (world record holder for traveling on inline skates) rode / skated this coaster track during its opening ceremony while carrying a liter of beer.
